title: Dune
creator: Frank Herbert
review: A masterpiece of world-building and sci-fi philosophy. The ecology of Arrakis, the spice, the Bene Gesserit order, and the exploration of messianic structures make this one of my favorite books of all time.

title: Project Hail Mary
creator: Andy Weir
review: Incredibly fun and scientifically detailed space survival story. The relationship between Ryland Grace and Rocky is one of the most heartwarming friendships in modern sci-fi. Page-turner from start to finish.

title: Gödel, Escher, Bach
creator: Douglas R. Hofstadter
review: A profound, playful exploration of self-reference, formal systems, art, and music. Reading it slowly to fully digest the concepts. The dialogues between Achilles and the Tortoise are brilliant.

title: Inception
creator: Christopher Nolan
review: A masterclass in original sci-fi action. The layers of dreams, Hans Zimmer's iconic brass-heavy score, and the emotional core of Cobb trying to get back to his kids make it a classic. The practical effects still hold up incredibly well.

title: Severance
creator: Dan Erickson
review: An eerie, gripping corporate thriller with stellar art direction. The sterile Lumon offices contrast beautifully with the characters' dark emotional struggles. Ben Stiller's direction is precise and tense.

title: Interstellar
creator: Christopher Nolan
review: My favorite space epic. Christopher Nolan manages to combine relativity, black holes, and deep human emotion into a breathtaking journey. Hans Zimmer's organ score is absolutely monumental.

title: Breaking Bad
creator: Vince Gilligan
review: The gold standard of television drama. Walter White's descent from a desperate chemistry teacher to a ruthless drug kingpin is one of the most meticulously written character arcs in media history.

title: Dune: Part Two
creator: Denis Villeneuve
review: A visual and auditory triumph. Denis Villeneuve successfully adapts the complex second half of Frank Herbert's novel, delivering massive scale battles, complex political intrigue, and an immersive soundscape.

title: Succession
creator: Jesse Armstrong
review: A tragicomic look at corporate power dynamics and generational trauma. The writing is incredibly sharp, with insults that cut like knives and a stellar ensemble cast where everyone shines.

title: Dark
creator: Baran bo Odar
review: The most intricate time-travel story ever put to screen. It demands your absolute attention, tracing multiple generations across multiple timelines. A masterpiece of puzzle-box television.

title: The Matrix
creator: Lana Wachowski, Lilly Wachowski
review: A landmark film that redefined sci-fi action cinema. Philosophy, cyberculture, and martial arts blended together with groundbreaking visual effects. A perfect film from start to finish.
